search_queries_used: site:bad.org.uk alopecia areata living guideline 2024 assessment follow-up
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic alopecia-areata severity or activity score
automatic topical, systemic, intralesional, or referral plan
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Extent, activity, pattern, age, comorbidities, and patient preference require direct assessment.
No treatment or referral was generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults and children with clinician-established alopecia areata; recommendations vary by age and severity.
UAE_applicability: BAD evidence requires UAE dermatology, medicine, laboratory, psychological-support, referral, and facility adaptation.
recency_verification: The BAD 2024 living guideline was reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The registered 2024 living guideline remains the selected current source.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The BAD living guideline supports documenting alopecia-areata extent, activity, pattern, symptoms, nail or scalp context, psychosocial impact, and change over time without grading automatically.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports full history including site, type, extent, stability, onset, progression, triggers, quality of life, psychological impact, family autoimmune history, photography, severity assessment, and specialist referral criteria.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The management context supports recording clinician-completed discussion, questions, an entered plan, and arranged follow-up without generating treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led assessment, shared priorities, severity tracking, expectation discussion, treatment selection, monitoring, and patient information without authorising autonomous treatment output.

unresolved_source_gaps: Pattern, scalp and body sites, extent and activity, nail findings, photographs, diagnosis confirmation, comorbidities, psychological impact, prior and current treatment, monitoring, clinician plan, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
